title: Repo cleanup: delete .tgz + review artifacts, gitignore *.tgz
description: profpowell-bg-wc-0.2.0.tgz (38KB binary) is committed at repo root — leftover from manual npm pack. .gitignore doesn't exclude *.tgz. Also remove obsolete review markdowns (claude-review-528.md and review-codex-528.md) after their content is filed as beads. Add *.tgz to .gitignore.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix dangling customElements pointer in published package
description: package.json:64 declares '"customElements": "custom-elements.json"' but files: ["dist","README.md","LICENSE"] excludes that file from the published tarball. Consumers' tooling (VS Code lit-plugin, etc.) follows the pointer to a 404. Pick one: (a) add 'custom-elements.json' to files array, (b) move the manifest under dist/ and update the pointer, or (c) drop the customElements field entirely.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add CI workflow (lint + build + test, Node 20/22)
description: .github/workflows/ contains only deploy-pages.yml; no PR gate runs tests, lint, or lib build. playwright.config.js declares forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI and retries gated on CI but nothing sets CI=1 for them — dead code. Add ci.yml with Node 20 + 22 matrix running: npm ci, npm run lint, npm run build (lib), npm run build:site, npx playwright install --with-deps chromium, npm test. Wire into branch protection.

title: Add per-preset visual regression + targeted lifecycle tests
description: Two gaps combined. (a) 54 of 56 presets untested: smoke.spec.js and alias.spec.js only exercise mesh-gradient (plus dither once). Generate a spec from the registry that mounts each preset on a fixed-size canvas, waits for ready, asserts a screenshot baseline at a loose pixel threshold. ~3MB of baselines. (b) Targeted lifecycle regressions: removeAttribute('preset') stops RAF and disposes context; unknown preset settles ready; context-unavailable settles ready; CEM contains canvas cssPart on bg-wc; pack-install resolves the documented subpath import.

title: Fix broken documented subpath import in docs/api.html
description: docs/api.html:426 example imports '@profpowell/bg-wc/presets/index.js'. With package.json exports '"./presets/*": "./dist/presets/*.js"', the resolver substitutes * with 'index.js' yielding './dist/presets/index.js.js' — a 404. Fix: drop the .js suffix in the doc snippet so it reads '@profpowell/bg-wc/presets/index'. Add a pack-install smoke test to lock it down.
close_reason: Closed

title: Dispose renderer when preset attribute removed
description: src/bg-wc.js:287 — #loadCurrentPreset returns immediately when this.preset is empty: 'if (!name) return'. So removeAttribute('preset') does NOT dispose the instance, cancel RAF, clear the canvas, or restore the fallback slot. Consumers using the attribute as a play/stop toggle silently leak a WebGL context (toward the ~16-context cap). Fix in attributeChangedCallback for preset: when newV is empty/null, call #disposeInstance(), cancel #rafId, clear canvas, emit bg-wc:preset-changed {from, to: null}.
close_reason: Closed

title: Settle el.ready on every failure path in #loadCurrentPreset
description: src/bg-wc.js:285-339. #resetReady() called at L289. All three catch blocks — loadPreset rejection (L294-298), context creation failure (L315-318), loaded.create() throw (L334-338) — emit bg-wc:error and return without calling #readyResolve(). Any caller awaiting el.ready after a typo'd preset, WebGL-disabled env, or malformed preset module hangs forever. Pick: reject ready with the error, or resolve with {ok:false, error}. Pin contract with a test.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix speed double-application across ~17 presets
description: Host scales time by speed at src/bg-wc.js:506 (this.#timeS += dt * params.speed), then ~17 presets derive their own dt = t - lastT and multiply by params.speed AGAIN. Net effect: speed^2 — at speed=2 they actually run at 4x. Affected: marquee.js:41, matrix.js:60, crawl.js:30, incoming.js:90, cascade.js:55, sinescroll.js:35, mystify.js:53, tetris.js:157, pulse.js:24, wireframe.js:23, network.js:42, asteroids.js:61,66, dashboard.js:28, trades.js:81, spirograph.js:23-28. Pick ONE: (a) stop pre-scaling in host and document t as raw seconds, or (b) audit each preset and remove the second multiplication. Option (a) is smaller diff.
close_reason: Per-preset audit (option b): removed second * params.speed from 13 presets that double-applied (crawl, incoming, marquee, sinescroll, mystify, matrix, cascade, wireframe, spirograph, pulse, tetris, dashboard, trades). asteroids and network kept as-is — they use params.speed as a per-frame scalar with no t/dt involvement (separate frame-rate-dependence bug, out of scope). Added contract comment at bg-wc.js:525.

title: border-wc CSS-first refresh: visual quality follow-up
description:
PR #7 (https://github.com/ProfPowell/border-wc/pull/7) shipped 19 effects with the CSS-first refresh, but user assessment after viewing live was 'not great'. Specifics not yet captured — next session should:

1. Walk the live gallery (https://profpowell.github.io/border-wc/docs/) effect-by-effect with the user OR via screenshots
2. For each weak effect, ask: is it salvageable with knob/palette tuning, or does it need a different technique?
3. Particular suspects (educated guesses from the refresh):
   - aurora: conic gradient may look too 'designer' without the right palette / thickness
   - barber: stripes may be too thick or move too fast at default speed
   - chroma: 1px shadows may be too subtle on dark backgrounds
   - wings: mix-blend-mode multiply against the demo background may be muddy
   - neon: feGaussianBlur stdDev=6 may be too soft or too bright
   - marquee: chase wave timing may need tuning
   - lightning/sparks refactors: verify they still read as bolts/comet vs. amorphous glow
4. Compare side-by-side with the reference codepens
5. Consider: should we add a per-effect 'preview against multiple backgrounds' affordance to the gallery?

Branch the next iteration off main.
close_reason: ak6: all 6 children complete; btc: being handled elsewhere

title: Demos hub: overlay context isolation + force-reload (visible LOST contexts on live)
description: Live still showed ~43 'too many WebGL' warnings and trench-run's main bg LOST despite the IO. Two compounding causes: (a) rootMargin 150px kept ~8 live during scroll churn; (b) browser-window's _reloadIframeIfNeeded only reloads if the iframe is EMPTY — a previously-loaded iframe with a lost context stays lost when maximized. Fix: rootMargin -> '0px' (visible-only); on click, unmount-all-other tiles + force-reload this tile (remove+set src) so the overlay opens with a guaranteed-fresh context; on overlay close, remount currently-visible tiles.
close_reason: Fixed (PR #30): rootMargin 0 + on-click unmount-others + force-reload this + remount-visible on close. Local-verified trench-run overlay renders (both canvases live, not lost).

title: Demos hub: bound WebGL contexts (lazy-mount/unmount iframes) — fixes blank demos
description: Root cause (systematic-debug confirmed): all 32 demo iframes load simultaneously (79 bg-wc, many WebGL) -> 'Too many active WebGL contexts. Oldest context will be lost' (45 warnings) -> WebGL demos blank intermittently (popart, trench-run main bg); Canvas2D ones (desktop bliss) survive. Fix: browser-window tiles carry data-demo-src (no src initially); IntersectionObserver sets src when the tile nears the viewport and removes it (releasing the context via _renderStructure) when it leaves. Keeps live contexts ~= visible set; browser drops the unloaded off-screen ones. Ensure-loaded on click before toggleMaximize.
close_reason: Fixed (PR #29). IntersectionObserver lazy-mounts/unmounts demo iframes -> 32 -> ~6 live; visible demos render (popart 3 canvases, desktop 1, trench-run 2 — all live, none lost); trench-run overlay shows the full vector trench (was blank). Too-many-WebGL warnings down from 45 to 20 transient. Click-to-maximize ensure-mounts first.

title: VB base tokens: borders + scrollbars + cursors (Decorated Layers #2)
description: Base tier in vanilla-breeze: CSS-first token categories for animated borders (spin/pulse/march/hue-cycle/breathe/trace via data-border attribute), scrollbars (scrollbar-color/width), cursors (custom + opt-in JS spotlight). Ships the COMPLETE border token vocabulary incl. extreme-only names. Reduced-motion contract. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-vb-base-tokens-borders-scrollbars-cursors-design.md. Target repo: vanilla-breeze.
acceptance_criteria: data-border base values render via VB CSS; scrollbar+cursor tokens themeable on :root; reduced-motion zeroes animations; specimen demo (port of borders-cursors-scrollbars.html); complete token set shipped.
notes: Implementation complete; PR open: https://github.com/ProfPowell/vanilla-breeze/pull/1 (vanilla-breeze, base work/2026-05-21). Awaiting merge.
close_reason: Closed

title: Decorated Layers — base-in-VB + extreme packages across visual surfaces
description: Umbrella epic. Family of platform-first, theme-aware decoration systems across surfaces (effect/background/border/scrollbar/cursor) in two strata: base (vanilla-breeze tokens/CSS) + extreme (opt-in packages). VB ships the complete token vocabulary and the base; packages embed the base for standalone. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-decorated-layers-design.md. Settled decisions: data-* attributes (data-border/data-background) as author hooks; data-background canonical (data-bg alias); background gets a light CSS base in VB; geometry authored in VB + embedded; spotlight in VB base; 'Decorated Layers' is internal-only. Sub-project #1 (umbrella) + specs #2/#4 are written.
close_reason: ak6: all 6 children complete; btc: being handled elsewhere

title: README polish: Highlights heading, link to full list, deprecation timeline
description: README.md:36-46 shows 9 of 56 presets in a table headed as if exhaustive — hero contradicts itself ('Dozens of presets…'). Rename to 'Highlights' and add a link to docs/api.html#presets underneath. Migration section needs a deprecation timeline ('gl-wc aliases removed in 1.0') so consumers know urgency.

title: Document the preset contract in src/presets/index.js
description: The preset shape — {resize(W,H), frame(t, params), staticFrame(params)?, dispose()?} — and the params catalog (palette/intensity/speed/density/seed/quality/fit/text) live implicitly across src/bg-wc.js:326-339, 467, 492, 508. Half the cross-preset inconsistencies (staticFrame duplication, speed double-app, unused uniforms) trace back to no written contract. Add a header comment block to src/presets/index.js spelling out the interface AND the time-scaling rule (whichever direction issue gl-wc-? lands on).

title: CEM hygiene: drift check, fix gl-wc→bg-wc in plugin, strip noise
description: Three CEM fixes. (a) custom-elements-manifest.config.mjs:24 keys cssParts on tagName === 'gl-wc' — after the rename, the canonical bg-wc element has no part='canvas' doc. Move cssParts to bg-wc (or apply to both while alias is supported). (b) Add 'cem analyze && git diff --exit-code custom-elements.json' to CI so the committed 50KB manifest can't drift silently. (c) Generated manifest leaks implementation noise: internal DOM-construction artifacts (textContent, className, slot name) and a generic event named 't'. Extend the plugin to strip these and explicitly declare public events (bg-wc:ready, bg-wc:error, bg-wc:preset-changed, bg-wc:visibility + legacy twins).

title: Pin engines.node, add build.sourcemap to vite.config
description: package.json lacks engines field — after the Node 22 globSync incident, pin engines.node: '>=20' so consumers/contributors fail fast. vite.config.js omits build.sourcemap, making upstream debugging harder than necessary. Add 'sourcemap: true'.

title: SSR guard for customElements.define at module load
description: src/bg-wc.js:518-539 and src/data-background.js call customElements.define at module load with no 'typeof customElements !== "undefined"' guard. Importing in a Node/SSR context throws. Wrap both define() calls in a feature-detect.

title: Move docs/border-idea/ and docs/superpowers/ out of deployed site
description: Both directories ship inside dist-site/ (the deployed Pages site). docs/border-idea/ is draft work for a sibling border-wc package; docs/superpowers/ holds 25 internal plans/specs (border-wc work, rename plan, etc.). Move to internal/ or .dev/ at repo root (or split border-idea into a sibling repo). Exclude from vite site build so dist-site/ stays public-clean.

title: Rewrite or retire spec.md (38 stale gl-wc references)
description: spec.md still titled <gl-wc>, has 38 'gl-wc' occurrences, package shown as @profpowell/gl-wc, events as gl-wc:*, CSS vars as --gl-wc-*, and claims 'Ship with nine presets' (actual: 56). References non-existent admin/handoffs/ path. README links to it as 'the full design'. Pick: (a) global rename pass + drop §5 preset list (point to docs/api.html#presets) + drop §10 docs outline + §12 now-answered open questions, or (b) replace with a thin DESIGN.md and update README to point at docs/api.html as canonical reference.

title: Fix battery observer cleanup race + make power-save observable
description: Two related issues. (a) observeBatteryPowerSave (src/util/pause.js:3-18, called from src/bg-wc.js:222-223) returns an async disposer. If element disconnects before navigator.getBattery() resolves, disconnectedCallback empties #cleanups BEFORE the disposer is pushed → battery listeners leak. Fix with AbortController or guard the .then() with isConnected. (b) power-save is read once on connect (bg-wc.js:65-79); not in observedAttributes, so runtime toggles are ignored. Add it and re-bind the observer on change.

title: Reorder #tick to schedule next frame after work
description: src/bg-wc.js:501-515 calls requestAnimationFrame(this.#tick) at the TOP of #tick. On error, the catch cancels #rafId but the frame was already enqueued, so it fires on a disposed/broken instance. Move the RAF schedule to the END of #tick, after the try/catch.

title: Review repository and document observations
description: Review the repository structure, implementation, tests, and documentation, then add a root-level review-codex-528.md file with observations and suggestions.
close_reason: Added review-codex-528.md with repository observations and suggestions.

title: Demos overlay: lock background scroll + stop in-iframe back-link nesting
description: Two bugs with the demos hub browser-window overlay: (1) when a window is maximized, wheel scrolls the page behind it (no scroll lock); fix: lock document scroll while any browser-window.browser-window-maximized exists, restore on close (MutationObserver in demos-hub.js). (2) each demo's '← Demos' back link (href=./) loads the hub INSIDE the iframe -> infinite nesting; fix: add target=_top to demo back-links so it navigates the top window to the hub (harmless standalone: _top==self).
close_reason: Fixed (PR #28): (1) background scroll-lock while a browser-window is maximized (html overflow:hidden via MutationObserver, restored on close) — verified wheel over modal keeps scrollY=0; (2) demo back-links get target=_top so '← Demos' in the overlay iframe navigates the top window to the hub instead of nesting — verified iframed back link target=_top. No console errors.

title: Demos hub: click tile opens browser-window overlay (toggleMaximize), not navigate
description: Correction: the grid tiles should be live browser-windows that, on click, OPEN as a maximized browser-window overlay (the component's toggleMaximize() => fixed 90vw/90vh modal with backdrop+Escape), showing the live demo in-page. Currently the overlay link navigates to the full demo page. Keep the same grid size/approach + scroll-passthrough; only change the click: delegated handler on .bw-open => preventDefault + browser-window.toggleMaximize(). Keep href as no-JS/modified-click fallback.
close_reason: Click-to-open-overlay shipped (PR #27). Clicking a tile calls browser-window.toggleMaximize() => live demo opens as a 90vw/90vh modal overlay (backdrop+Escape) in-page, instead of navigating. Same grid + scroll-passthrough preserved; href fallback for no-JS/cmd-click. Headless-verified: wheel scrolls page, click maximizes (aria-modal, covers viewport, backdrop), Escape closes, no errors.

title: Demos hub: convert entire grid to browser-window previews + scroll fix
description: User: browser-window approach is much better, do it for all demos; ensure the page scrolls, not the iframes. Replace all 32 grid cards (and fold in the 2 featured) with <browser-window src> previews of the real demos. Scroll fix: transparent overlay link over each window so wheel scrolls the page and click opens the full demo (in-grid windows are non-interactive previews). Lazy-loading is built into browser-window (loading=lazy). Remove dead per-card bg-wc preview palettes + .preview/.label/a.demo CSS + the Featured section.
close_reason: All demos converted to browser-window previews (PR #26). 32 <browser-window src> tiles of the real demos in one grid; transparent overlay link makes the page scroll (wheel 0->800 verified) instead of the iframe, click opens the full demo; iframes loading=lazy. Dead preview CSS removed. Headless-verified, no console errors.

title: Demos hub: featured browser-window demos (iframed, phase 2)
description: Phase 2 of demos-page work. Adopt browser-window approach B (iframed real demo, chosen after A/B comparison). Add a 'Featured' section atop the demos hub with 2 <browser-window src=...> demos (cyberpunk + stripe-wave) loading the real full-bleed demo pages with source-view/expand. Keep the existing card grid below. browser-window added to demos-hub.js. Remove the scratch comparison pages (_bw-wrap/_bw-iframe/_bw-test).
close_reason: Featured browser-window demos shipped (PR #25, approach B). Demos hub now has a Featured section with 2 iframed <browser-window> demos (cyberpunk + stripe-wave) above the grid; @profpowell/browser-window added as a dep. Scratch comparison pages removed. Headless-verified: 2 featured BWs render real demos, 32 grid cards intact, no console errors.

title: Demos hub: adopt the standard site header + theme picker
description: demos/index.html is bespoke (hardcoded dark, custom .topbar, no VB). Make it a first-class site page: load VB + site.css + theme-picker + prefer-dark, replace .topbar with the standard <header class=site> (Demos current), reconcile page-chrome styles to VB tokens (map --surface/--border/--accent to VB), keep the demo grid + per-card preview palettes fixed. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-26-demos-hub-site-header-design.md. Phase 1 of 2 (phase 2 = browser-window prototype).
close_reason: Demos hub now uses the standard site header + theme picker (PR #24). Loads VB + site.css + prefer-dark via demos-hub.js; .topbar replaced by <header class=site> (Demos current); page chrome on VB theming; grid + per-card palettes unchanged. Headless-verified identical to gallery; no console errors. Phase 1 done; phase 2 (browser-window) separate.

title: Remove dead Palette control from gallery (works on only 5/55 presets)
description: User: the Palette pulldown on docs/index.html appears to do nothing (overridden by theme). Investigation: params.palette is honored by only 5 presets (crawl/marquee/cascade/sinescroll/particles); the other 50 ignore it, so it reads as dead. Decision: remove the control + its gallery.js wiring. The 5 presets fall back to their default 'theme' path (bg-wc #readParams defaults palette to 'theme' when the attr is absent). Preset code left intact (rainbow/mono branches become unreachable but harmless).
close_reason: Removed the dead Palette control (PR #23). It was honored by only 5/55 presets and read as broken; deleted the pulldown + gallery.js wiring. Motion control unchanged. Headless-verified: control gone, no console errors, gallery renders.

title: Strip marketing hero from api.html docs (get straight to the docs)
description: User: api.html shouldn't have a marketing hero at top; get straight to the docs (template: profpowell.github.io/browser-console/api.html). Remove the data-background hero (eyebrow/h1/lede/chips), add a plain 'API Reference' h1 + one-line desc at the top of <main>, delete now-dead hero CSS (.hero[data-background], .eyebrow, .lede, .chip, .meta-row). Keep the sticky site header/nav/theme-picker; binder still demoed by the data-background section below.
close_reason: Removed api.html marketing hero (PR #22); page now leads with a plain 'API Reference' title and goes straight to the docs, matching the browser-console template. Dead hero CSS deleted. Headless-verified: no hero, h1 at top, site nav intact, no console errors.

title: Shared perimeter/shape geometry primitive (Decorated Layers #3)
description: perimeterPath()/perimeterSampler() for an element's outer shape (rounded rects MVP; shape()/path() later). Authored once in vanilla-breeze ('the shape'), embedded into bg-wc/border-wc for standalone. border-wc strokes it; bg-wc masks shaders to it. Spec ref: umbrella §4 + border-wc spec §4. Target: vanilla-breeze (+embedded).
notes: Implementation complete; PR open: https://github.com/ProfPowell/vanilla-breeze/pull/2 (vanilla-breeze, base work/2026-05-21). 16 unit tests; source-only. Awaiting merge.
close_reason: Closed

title: border-wc component — extreme border effects (Decorated Layers #4)
description: New sibling component @profpowell/border-wc (new repo ~/src/border-wc). Extreme tier only: squiggle (SVG), draw (SVG), sparks (Canvas), liquid (WebGL, deferred). CSS effects (gradient/glow/dash) delegate to VB base (#2) as spin/pulse/march — not reimplemented. Consumes the shared perimeter primitive (#3); embeds VB base for standalone. oklch-safe color parsing (pixel readback). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-border-wc-component-design.md.
acceptance_criteria: border-wc.js dispatcher + squiggle/draw/sparks working; reduced-motion guard; IntersectionObserver pause for sparks; sibling-standard toolchain; published as @profpowell/border-wc.
notes:
Implementation complete + verified in local repo ~/src/border-wc (branch main, ~12 commits): element+dispatcher, draw/squiggle/sparks effects, embedded perimeter, oklch-safe canvas color, reduced-motion, 8 Playwright specs pass, lint clean, cem manifest, demo renders all 3 effects. No GitHub remote yet — awaiting publish decision.
Published: https://github.com/ProfPowell/border-wc (public). CI green (lint/audit/test/build); Pages live + verified: https://profpowell.github.io/border-wc/ (all 3 effects render, no errors). npm publish is a later step.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make demo navigation consistent across gallery, docs, and demos
description:
User reports that clicking through to the demo pages is 'not consistent.' Investigate and unify navigation:
- The gallery (docs/index.html) and api.html link to '../demos/' (the demos index). Confirm demos/index.html links back to the gallery/docs consistently and that all 32 demo pages have a consistent way back (currently bespoke per-aesthetic, many may have no nav at all).
- Decide on a consistent pattern: a minimal, unobtrusive 'back to gallery' affordance on each demo that doesn't break the bespoke aesthetic, plus a consistent demos/index.html that links to gallery + docs.
- Verify all cross-links resolve (no 404s) from demos/ -> ../docs/ etc.
acceptance_criteria: Navigation between gallery, docs, and demos is consistent and discoverable; every demo has a consistent way back to the gallery; demos/index.html links to gallery + docs; no broken cross-links.
close_reason: Consistent demo nav shipped (PR #21). Fixed demos-hub broken links (Docs-> now reaches api.html, was the gallery; Gallery-> direct not via redirect); normalized all 32 per-demo back-links to '← Demos' keeping bespoke styling; refreshed stale root redirect copy; unified gallery+api site headers (Gallery/Docs/Demos/GitHub + aria-current). Headless crawl: all links 200, no 404s, no console errors. 12/12 component tests unaffected.

title: Docs hero: default dark + make the data-bg animation visible around the glass panel
description:
The hero text now sits in a themed glass panel for contrast (done), but two issues remain on the API docs hero (docs/api.html):

1. The API page defaults to light (theme-picker auto) while the gallery (docs/index.html) defaults to dark via preferDarkByDefault() in docs/gallery.js. Give api.html the same first-load dark nudge for consistency (it shares docs-entry.js, which has no such logic).
2. With the centered glass panel, the data-bg='mesh-gradient' animation is barely visible (washed out, especially in light). Rework so the animation actually showcases the component — e.g. offset/left-align the panel like the gallery hero so the animation reads alongside, and/or bump data-bg-intensity. Keep the themed-scrim contrast guarantee.
acceptance_criteria: API docs hero defaults to dark on first load (matching the gallery); the data-bg animation is clearly visible around/alongside the text panel in both light and dark; text contrast remains guaranteed in all themes.
close_reason: Full-bleed docs hero shipped (PR #20). Both heroes edge-to-edge with offset glass panel aligned to content column (animation reads beside text); api.html gets first-load dark parity via shared docs/prefer-dark.js; api intensity 0.65->0.72. Headless-verified: dark mode, full width, panel offset 144px, scrim intact light+dark, no console errors. 12/12 component tests unaffected.

title: Core polish batch (time-base, computed-style, dual-emit, fit, shader log)
description: Misc src/bg-wc.js polish. (1) #evalPlay uses performance.now() but #tick uses rAF nowMs (L484, L501) — one-frame dt spike on resume; consistent time base. (2) #readParams calls getComputedStyle(this) every frame (L409) — add a frame-local cache. (3) #emit fires every event twice (canonical + legacy gl-wc:) — gate the legacy twin behind a build flag for v0.3 cleanup. (4) 'fit' attribute is observed and triggers #resize (L77, L269) but #resize never applies it — presets must honor it themselves; document or implement. (5) src/renderer/webgl.js:18-23 shader compile errors only surface as host runtime errors; the GL log isn't echoed to console.error — add it on throw for prod debuggability. (6) Suppress the vanilla-breeze.css.map warning during npm test.

title: Staticframe consistency across presets
description: Inconsistent staticFrame implementations. Some presets duplicate draw logic (confetti, fireworks, matrix, tetris); others just delegate to frame(0, params) (snow, plasma, conic, mesh-gradient, aurora, dither, comic, particles). Prefer delegation unless the preset genuinely needs a different still image. Audit + simplify the duplicated ones.

title: tokens.js color cache: add LRU cap
description: src/renderer/tokens.js:15 cache grows unbounded. Themed apps with many dynamic colors could grow it indefinitely (memory leak in long-lived SPAs). Add a small LRU cap (~128 entries).

title: Add pack-install smoke test to CI
description: Given the custom exports map with './presets/*', a CI step that runs 'npm pack' and installs into a tmp fixture, then dynamically imports '@profpowell/bg-wc/presets/dither', would catch dist-layout breaks, missing files in 'files' array, or wrong subpath patterns before publish. Add alongside the new ci.yml workflow.

title: Fold test/ into lint+format; add Playwright globals
description: eslint.config.js:40 ignores test/; format script only globs 'src/**/*.js' (package.json:49) — tests are neither linted nor formatted. Lint test/ too (same JS, no reason to skip); add Playwright globals (test, expect, etc.) to the eslint globals config. Also fold test/ into the prettier glob.

title: Fill in CLAUDE.md build & architecture sections
description: CLAUDE.md has placeholder 'Build & Test' ('_Add your build and test commands here_') and 'Architecture Overview' sections. An agent has to discover everything from scratch. Fill in: commands (npm run dev, npm run build, npm run build:site, npm test, npm run analyze); architecture pointers (src/bg-wc.js main element, src/presets/ registry, src/renderer/ thin wrappers); the ~16 WebGL-context cap as a first-class constraint shaping the demos hub.

title: Per-preset bugfix batch (matrix, mesh-gradient, dither, wireframe, fireworks, grain)
description: Collected per-preset fixes from cross-review. (1) matrix.js:82 resize() rebuilds with hardcoded density:0.5 — discards user's density on every container resize. (2) mesh-gradient.js:55-69 declares u_density in shader but never passes params.density — density attribute silently ignored. (3) dither.js declares u_bg uniform but FS body never reads it — dead uniform. (4) wireframe.js has no dispose and allocates ~880 small arrays per frame at default seg/lines — preallocate. (5) fireworks.js:31 uses unbounded module-scoped 'cycle++'; line 111 hardcodes white for core dot — should use c.fg. (6) grain.js doesn't consume getColors() — intentional? Either document or tint via fg.

title: Forward DPR (pxScale) into preset context; fix hi-DPI thinness
description: Host sizes canvas at devicePixelRatio (src/bg-wc.js:457-462), but canvas2d presets receive raw W,H. matrix glyph step, asteroids/fireworks line widths, wireframe strokes are device-pixel constants — they look thin on hi-DPI displays. Forward pxScale via the preset context (create({...pxScale})). Update line-width / glyph-size sites in asteroids, fireworks, wireframe, matrix, mystify, network.

title: Add makeShaderPreset helper; collapse ~30 WebGL presets
description: ~25 lines of identical setup repeated across ~30 shader presets (plasma, conic, mesh-gradient, aurora, dither, glitch, vhs, comic, etc.): createProgram → getAttribLocation('a_pos') → N × getUniformLocation → viewport/clearColor/clear/useProgram/bindQuad → N × uniform*f → drawArrays → dispose. Uniform vocabulary is nearly closed: u_time, u_intensity, u_density, u_c1..c3, u_bg, u_res. makeShaderPreset(fs, uniforms, mapFn) would cut ~750 LOC and reduce a new preset to its FS + 5 lines. Lives next to src/renderer/webgl.js.

title: Extract rgbCss/rgbaCss helpers to tokens.js
description: ~98 inline rgb()/rgba() string assemblies across canvas2d presets, plus 12 private 'function rgb(c)' copies: asteroids, cascade, crawl, dashboard, incoming, marquee, scatter, spirograph, sinescroll, vectormap, waveform, wordcloud. Centralize as rgbCss(c) / rgbaCss(c, a) in src/renderer/tokens.js. Mechanical replacement.

title: Publish @profpowell/bg-wc@0.2.0 to npm (first publish)
description: bg-wc was renamed from gl-wc (never published). First npm publish of the new name. User runs: npm login, then npm publish --access public from ~/src/gl-wc. Package verified publish-ready via dry-run (66 files, 38.9kB, includes dist/bg-wc.js + dist/data-background.js + presets + README + LICENSE).
close_reason: Published @profpowell/bg-wc@0.2.0 to npm (latest). Verified on registry: main=dist/bg-wc.js, exports ./ + ./data-background + ./data-bg + ./presets/*, 66 files.

title: Sync embedded perimeter.js copies (bg-wc, border-wc) with VB Phase 2
description: vanilla-breeze src/lib/perimeter.js gained the Phase 2 segment model + shape builders (ak6.6). bg-wc and border-wc embed a copy that still has only the uniform rounded-rect API. Sync the embedded copies when those consumers need non-uniform/clip-path shapes. Additive (existing roundedRect* signatures unchanged), so not urgent.
close_reason: Synced border-wc/src/perimeter.js to VB canonical Phase 2 (PR #2 merged, CI green, 16/16 tests). bg-wc embeds NO perimeter copy (backgrounds don't trace perimeters), so border-wc was the only copy. Uniform roundedRect* API unchanged; effects unaffected; Phase 2 builders additive.

title: npm deprecate @profpowell/gl-wc -> bg-wc
description: After publishing @profpowell/bg-wc@0.2.0, run: npm deprecate @profpowell/gl-wc "renamed to @profpowell/bg-wc". Manual step; needs npm auth. Follow-up from ak6.5.
close_reason: Moot: @profpowell/gl-wc was never published to npm (registry 404), so there is nothing to deprecate. The real action — first publish of @profpowell/bg-wc — is tracked separately (gl-wc-s5b).

title: perimeter: advanced shapes — asymmetric/elliptical radii, clip-path, non-rect (Decorated Layers, perimeter Phase 2)
description: High-value follow-up to ak6.2 (src/lib/perimeter.js). After the uniform-rounded-rect MVP lands, extend the perimeter primitive to the extreme shapes that show off border-wc/bg-wc: (1) asymmetric per-corner border-radius (8-value parse); (2) elliptical radii rx!=ry (arc-length over ellipse segments); (3) clip-path: shape()/path() tracing (read getComputedStyle(host).clipPath); (4) arbitrary polygons / clip-path: polygon(). The MVP API (perimeterPath/perimeterSampler + the t-contract) is designed to extend to these without breaking consumers. Spec §5 of docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-perimeter-geometry-ak6.2-design.md. Target: vanilla-breeze src/lib/perimeter.js.
close_reason: perimeter Phase 2 shipped in vanilla-breeze (PR #3 merged to work/2026-05-21): segment-model core + asymmetric/elliptical radii, clip-path polygon/circle/ellipse/inset/path()/shape(), shape-detecting DOM wrappers. 40/40 tests; uniform path reimplemented on core with byte-identical output.

title: gl-wc -> bg-wc rename + data-background alignment (Decorated Layers #6)
description: Breaking rename: @profpowell/gl-wc -> @profpowell/bg-wc; <gl-wc> -> <bg-wc>; --gl-wc-* -> --bg-wc-*; data-bg -> data-background (canonical) with data-bg/--gl-wc-* kept as deprecated aliases. Ship final gl-wc that subclass-aliases bg-wc with a deprecation warning. Sequenced late to avoid churn during border work. Spec ref: umbrella §7.
acceptance_criteria: @profpowell/bg-wc published; <gl-wc> aliases <bg-wc> with deprecation warning; data-bg + --gl-wc-* still work; docs/site updated to bg-wc.
close_reason: gl-wc->bg-wc rename shipped (PR #19 merged): <bg-wc> canonical, <gl-wc>/data-bg/--gl-wc-*/gl-wc:* deprecated aliases warn-once. @profpowell/bg-wc@0.2.0. GitHub repo renamed to bg-wc, Pages live at /bg-wc. 12/12 tests.

title: data-border binder — attribute layer for border-wc (Decorated Layers #5)
description: Mirror src/data-bg.js: scan + MutationObserver for data-border; base values are pure VB CSS (no injection), extreme values inject <border-wc effect=...>. Routes base->CSS, extreme->component. Spec ref: border-wc spec §6. Target: border-wc/gl-wc.
close_reason: data-border-effect binder shipped in border-wc (PR #1 merged): opt-in ./attr entry applies extreme effects to any annotated element; base values ignored. 15/15 tests, CI green.

title: Extract named constants for magic numbers
description: Several defensible-but-unnamed magic numbers in core. MAX_DT_S=0.1 (bg-wc.js:504), LOW_BATTERY_THRESHOLD=0.2 (pause.js:6), VISIBILITY_ROOT_MARGIN='200px' (observe.js:3), DEFAULT_DPR_CAP=2 (bg-wc.js:461). Extract to named constants at top of each file.

value: The package formerly @profpowell/gl-wc / <gl-wc> is now @profpowell/bg-wc / <bg-wc> (ak6.5, v0.2.0). GitHub repo renamed ProfPowell/gl-wc -> ProfPowell/bg-wc (local dir still ~/src/gl-wc). Legacy <gl-wc>, data-bg, --gl-wc-*, gl-wc:* events all still work as warn-once deprecated aliases. Canonical binder attribute is data-background.
